jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes into something during opening credits
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly integrated with character but predictable

jokes.text: I wanna visit a screen door factory
jokes.setup: Family excited about winning trip to Delaware
jokes.punchline: Delaware's pathetically mundane tourist attractions
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about Delaware's lack of appeal - specific and unexpected

jokes.text: Well, we are not boarding that plane unless you waive that tax. Waive it!
jokes.setup: Homer objects to $5 airport tax
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction to a trivial fee
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stubbornness over petty amounts - character-appropriate but predictable

jokes.text: Stupid anti-fist-shaking laws!
jokes.setup: Homer banned from airplane for protesting
jokes.punchline: Implies there are laws specifically against fist-shaking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - suggests a bizarrely specific law that perfectly captures bureaucratic overreach

jokes.text: Cool. A dead hobo.
jokes.setup: Family discovers what appears to be a corpse on train
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual enthusiasm about death
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's morbid curiosity - dark but character-appropriate

jokes.text: I'm not a stabbin' hobo. I'm a singin' hobo.
jokes.setup: Family fears dangerous hobo
jokes.punchline: Hobo clarifies his specialty in reassuring way
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - relieves tension with unexpected categorization of hobo types

jokes.text: # Stabbin' folks with my hobo knife #
jokes.setup: Hobo just claimed he's not a stabbing hobo
jokes.punchline: Immediately sings about stabbing people
jokes.explanation: Excellent immediate reversal of expectation - undermines his reassurance instantly

jokes.text: I was just having a little fun with you 'nobos.'
jokes.setup: Family wants less threatening song
jokes.punchline: Hobo's term for non-hobos
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau creating hobo insider terminology - economical and memorable

jokes.text: # His body grew big but his brain stayed small #
jokes.setup: Paul Bunyan's continued growth
jokes.punchline: Direct explanation of the visual contradiction
jokes.explanation: Straightforward explanation that works but lacks surprise or cleverness

jokes.text: Yuck! All right, uh, let's get started on lunch.
jokes.setup: Paul's flapjacks cause destruction and death
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact move to next meal despite catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through casual acceptance of disaster, good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Can anybody hear me? I think I found a way out. It's not pretty, but it'll do.
jokes.setup: Lenny is missing after Paul's flapjack eating
jokes.punchline: He's trapped inside Paul and found an escape route
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor handled cleverly through implication - audience fills in disgusting details

jokes.text: Same way we got rid of Laura Ingalls Wilder.
jokes.setup: Townspeople plan to get Paul drunk and drag him away
jokes.punchline: They previously used this method on beloved children's author
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of wholesome Little House imagery - darkly funny historical revision

jokes.text: Aw, you guys are the greatest friends a giant doofus could have.
jokes.setup: Townspeople offer Paul beer to manipulate him
jokes.punchline: Paul's innocent gratitude while calling himself a doofus
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness combined with complete obliviousness to manipulation

jokes.text: Good Lord! Brought down by one beer? And a couple of these babies.
jokes.setup: Paul passes out surprisingly easily from one beer
jokes.punchline: Reveals they also drugged him heavily
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing the real reason Paul collapsed

jokes.text: Paul Bunyan never fought Rodan.
jokes.setup: Hobo's tall tale includes Paul fighting a giant monster
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaks fourth wall to critique story accuracy
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual pedantry contrasts with hobo's creative license - character-perfect

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey! Who's the hobo here?
jokes.setup: Lisa critiques the story's accuracy
jokes.punchline: Hobo asserts his storytelling authority
jokes.explanation: Defends creative storytelling privileges based on hobo status - absurd logic

jokes.text: I just need a few more yoga classes.
jokes.setup: Paul asks when they'll be intimate
jokes.punchline: She needs yoga preparation for giant boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Clever solution to the logistics of giant-human romance

jokes.text: Pain in the glayvin!
jokes.setup: Meteor approaches Earth
jokes.punchline: Professor Frink's signature nonsense exclamation
jokes.explanation: Familiar character callback but feels forced in this context

jokes.text: God has sent this fiery kill rock to show us his love.
jokes.setup: Deadly meteor approaching
jokes.punchline: Reverend's backwards interpretation of divine intention
jokes.explanation: Good religious satire with 'fiery kill rock' being particularly effective phrasing

jokes.text: When I'm crushing and killing you, you don't like me. But when I can save your life, suddenly I'm Mr. Popular.
jokes.setup: Townspeople ask Paul to save them from meteor
jokes.punchline: Paul points out their hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Solid observation about situational friendship - relatable social commentary

jokes.text: This one's for the little crippled boy... that I crippled.
jokes.setup: Paul dedicates meteor hit like a baseball
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's responsible for the boy's condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection - starts sentimental then becomes horrifying

jokes.text: And that's how Paul Bunyan started the great Chicago fire.
jokes.setup: Paul successfully hits meteor away from town
jokes.punchline: His heroic act caused different historical disaster
jokes.explanation: Clever historical recontextualization - victory becomes different tragedy

jokes.text: But first, who wants to give me a sponge bath? I'm filthy.
jokes.setup: Family requests another story
jokes.punchline: Hobo makes uncomfortable personal request
jokes.explanation: Hobo taking advantage of captive audience - appropriately uncomfortable

jokes.text: But first, who wants to give me a sponge bath? I'm filthy.
jokes.setup: Family requests another story
jokes.punchline: Hobo's inappropriate personal hygiene request
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate request comedy - sets up running gag

jokes.text: Who put the beans in my bindle?
jokes.setup: Hobo does impression of other hobos
jokes.punchline: Hobo relationship drama over bean placement
jokes.explanation: Mundane domestic dispute in hobo context - mildly amusing premise

jokes.text: It's the tale ofJohnny- No. Connie Appleseed.
jokes.setup: Hobo starting new tale specifically for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Self-corrects to gender-swap classic American folk hero
jokes.explanation: Simple gender swap of familiar character for target audience member

jokes.text: Why'd you kill another one? Dessert.
jokes.setup: Homer kills second buffalo after first
jokes.punchline: Casual single-word justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Homer's simple food logic

jokes.text: Dad, you just killed a poor defenseless buffalo. A poor, delicious buffalo.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizes father for killing buffalo
jokes.punchline: Homer's moral regret immediately turns to appetite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - brief conscience immediately overruled by appetite

jokes.text: Wipe out an entire species? Why, that's impossible.
jokes.setup: Lisa warns about buffalo extinction
jokes.punchline: Historical ignorance about future reality
jokes.explanation: Historical irony about environmental destruction - audience knows better

jokes.text: Of course! Apples!
jokes.setup: Connie seeking renewable food source
jokes.punchline: Simple obvious solution presented as breakthrough
jokes.explanation: Straightforward revelation that sets up premise rather than being funny itself

jokes.text: Oh, boy! Buffalo testicles!
jokes.setup: Lisa shows family apples she found
jokes.punchline: Homer misidentifies them as buffalo genitals
jokes.explanation: Homer's gross enthusiasm for bizarre food - character-appropriate grossout humor

jokes.text: And her family changed theirs to Bufflekill.
jokes.setup: Connie changed her name to Appleseed
jokes.punchline: Family adopted name reflecting their buffalo slaughter
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel naming convention - simple but effective callback

jokes.text: how about whipping up some buffalo sausage... huevos buffaleros, and some fresh-squeezed buffal-O. J?
jokes.setup: Homer wants buffalo for breakfast
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous buffalo-based menu items
jokes.explanation: Good escalating wordplay - 'buffal-O.J.' is particularly clever portmanteau

jokes.text: Calm down, Pa. There's two left.
jokes.setup: Homer despairs about killing all buffalo
jokes.punchline: Bart helpfully points out remaining targets
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection - offers comfort that immediately enables more destruction

jokes.text: It's like a hootenanny in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Starving people taste apples for first time
jokes.punchline: Bizarre folksy description of flavor
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd taste description - 'hootenanny' is perfect period-appropriate word choice

jokes.text: So now we're not eating Homer?
jokes.setup: Townspeople saved from starvation by apples
jokes.punchline: Disappointed they can't cannibalize Homer
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical - casual disappointment about cancelled cannibalism

jokes.text: Apple wine, apple whiskey... apple schnapps, apple martinis... uh, Snapple with vodka in it... apple nail polish remover-
jokes.setup: Homer lists good things with apples
jokes.punchline: List devolves from alcohol to toxic substances
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's alcoholic priorities and complete loss of logic

jokes.text: Don't forget applesauce. Yeah. I suppose you could grind some pills into it.
jokes.setup: Marge adds innocent apple product
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately sees pharmaceutical application
jokes.explanation: Homer corrupts Marge's wholesome suggestion with drug delivery method

jokes.text: Yeah. I suppose you could grind some pills into it.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests applesauce
jokes.punchline: Homer finds way to make applesauce about drugs
jokes.explanation: Homer finds substance abuse angle in innocent suggestion - character-consistent

jokes.text: That's not a tall tale. It's a book by Mark Twain.
jokes.setup: Hobo announces Tom Sawyer and Huck Finn story
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out it's established literature, not folklore
jokes.explanation: Lisa as voice of literary accuracy, breaking fourth wall appropriately

jokes.text: That's not a tall tale. It's a book by Mark Twain.
jokes.setup: Hobo begins Tom Sawyer story
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out it's established literature
jokes.explanation: Lisa's pedantic accuracy again - consistent character trait

jokes.text: Whitewashing sucks, Tom. It powerful sucks.
jokes.setup: Tom Sawyer whitewashing scene setup
jokes.punchline: Huck's period-appropriate complaint with emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good period language with Bart's attitude shining through historical character

jokes.text: I'd just get a new neck off n a cat.
jokes.setup: Marge warns about breaking his neck
jokes.punchline: Huck's bizarre understanding of anatomy
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd child logic about body parts - captures Huck's resourcefulness

jokes.text: I was gonna let go by-and-by.
jokes.setup: Caught holding Becky's hand
jokes.punchline: Vague timeline excuse using period language
jokes.explanation: Good period language for teenage awkwardness around romance

jokes.text: When are you gonna put down that gun?
jokes.setup: Reference to their shotgun wedding
jokes.punchline: Grandpa still holding wedding gun years later
jokes.explanation: Absurd literalism - shotgun wedding continues indefinitely

jokes.text: Hey, they done switched the groom with a pig!
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony in progress
jokes.punchline: Groom has been replaced by pig unnoticed
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag - substitution comedy with delayed discovery

jokes.text: Hey, they done switched the groom with a pig!
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony proceeding
jokes.punchline: Discovers animal substitution
jokes.explanation: Classic bait-and-switch visual gag with period appropriate language

jokes.text: No wonder he was pooping so much.
jokes.setup: Discovery that groom was replaced with pig
jokes.punchline: Retroactive explanation for suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that makes narrative sense - explains overlooked red flag

jokes.text: Please do not exit the raft... until it has come to a complete stop.
jokes.setup: Tom and Huck's raft going over waterfall
jokes.punchline: Modern safety announcement in 1800s setting
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic humor - modern bureaucracy in historical adventure

jokes.text: fatback, hardtack, fat pone, 'corntack.' Any 'tackback'? 'Tackback'? I mean 'backtack.'
jokes.setup: Tom lists their remaining food supplies
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical food name combinations
jokes.explanation: Wordplay becomes nonsensical - period food terms jumbled into meaninglessness

jokes.text: Double dang!
jokes.setup: Problems with food storage
jokes.punchline: Period-appropriate escalated frustration
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of period curse word, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Aw, donkey butter!
jokes.setup: Store owner drops food
jokes.punchline: Period-appropriate euphemism curse
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd period curse word - 'donkey butter' is memorably ridiculous

jokes.text: Well, dog my cats. They's dis-apporated.
jokes.setup: Chaser can't find Tom and Huck
jokes.punchline: Folksy malapropism for 'disappeared'
jokes.explanation: Good period dialect humor - 'dis-apporated' is clever malapropism

jokes.text: This ain't no five-X whiskey. I can still see.
jokes.setup: Patron orders strong whiskey
jokes.punchline: Measures alcohol strength by vision impairment
jokes.explanation: Excellent period drinking humor - logical but absurd quality measurement

jokes.text: Now let's just put 'em away. Nobody here is a cheat.
jokes.setup: Multiple people drawing guns over cheating accusation
jokes.punchline: Futile attempt at peace while acknowledging the trigger word
jokes.explanation: Sets up inevitable escalation by saying the forbidden word

jokes.text: Man, those derringer bullets are weak. Powerful weak.
jokes.setup: Bar shootout with period weapons
jokes.punchline: Commentary on ineffective small caliber weapons
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate observation about weapon inadequacy - mild historical humor

jokes.text: And that was Tom and Huck's last adventure.
jokes.setup: Tom and Huck's funeral shown
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact announcement of protagonists' death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in casually announcing beloved characters' deaths

jokes.text: We're gonna visit the place where JCPenney sends their damaged merchandise.
jokes.setup: Family invites hobo to see Delaware with them
jokes.punchline: Delaware's only attraction is retail damage center
jokes.explanation: Continues Delaware mockery with specific corporate detail - good callback

jokes.text: swappin' stories for sponge baths.
jokes.setup: Hobo's future plans
jokes.punchline: Returns to established transactional relationship
jokes.explanation: Pays off earlier setup but doesn't add much new humor

jokes.text: Oh, boy! Buffalo testicles!
jokes.setup: End credits scene
jokes.punchline: Homer still thinks apples are buffalo testicles
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier joke - competent but uninspired tag

jokes.text: Oh, boy! Buffalo testicles! [Chomping Sound]
jokes.setup: Credits sequence callback
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically eating what he mistook for apples
jokes.explanation: Crude callback that provides closure to earlier gag but relies on gross-out humor
